name: Serverless Function Abuse Detection
description: Detects abuse of serverless functions (Lambda, Cloud Functions, Azure Functions). Riggs AI analyzes invocation patterns for cryptomining, data exfiltration, or unauthorized code execution. Confirmed abuse triggers function throttling, IAM role review, forensic investigation of invocation logs, incident ticket, and team notification. False positives receive a severity update and analyst note.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: AWS Security Hub
required_integrations.reason: Source of serverless anomaly findings and Lambda configuration management

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create incident tickets for confirmed serverless abuse

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify the cloud security team of serverless abuse and response actions

config.description: Triggers when anomalous serverless function behavior is detected such as unusual invocation rates, unexpected outbound connections, or high resource consumption

config.additional_instructions: Analyze serverless function abuse indicators: unusual invocation spikes, outbound data transfer, execution duration anomalies, unauthorized dependencies
config.description: AI-powered analysis of serverless function behavior to determine if abuse such as cryptomining, data exfiltration, or unauthorized code execution is occurring
Routes based on whether the AI assessment confirmed serverless function abuse
Set reserved concurrency to zero to effectively disable the abused function while preserving it for forensic analysis

config.query: IAM role policies for execution_role={{trigger_1.alert.execution_role}}: attached policies, inline policies, recent role assumption events, cross-service permissions
config.description: Review the IAM execution role attached to the serverless function to identify overprivileged permissions or recent suspicious role changes

config.query: CloudWatch Logs for function={{trigger_1.alert.function_name}} in the last 24 hours: invocation count, error rate, outbound connections, payload sizes
config.description: Pull invocation logs and metrics to build a forensic timeline of the abuse activity
Create an incident ticket with complete forensic details of the serverless abuse
Notify the cloud security team of the serverless abuse and all containment actions

parameters.note: Reviewed by Riggs AI (cloud_security template). Serverless anomaly determined non-malicious — likely legitimate usage spike. Confidence: {{analyze_1.confidence}}. Assessment: {{analyze_1.summary}}

config.description: Document that AI analysis did not confirm serverless abuse
Serverless function abuse detection and response complete
